textPlain: Silly question. Are there any 486-compatible small CPUs that could be embedded into a project instead of using an FPGA? Given that AMD, Intel and others have the ability to make 486-compatible processors currently, I would have thought you could just buy a CPU or SoC to run 486 code.
Doesn’t DDR just stand for Double-Data-Rate? So you implemented basic DDR on top is sdram. Not a bad approach, just wanted to point it out.
I miss Intel's Quark chips. Tiny, cheap, and Pentium enough.
486? If it has VLB, it can play DOOM well!
